title: Strukov

infobox.value: is a municipality and village in [Olomouc District](/wiki/Olomouc_District "Olomouc District") in the [Olomouc Region](/wiki/Olomouc_Region "Olomouc Region") of the [Czech Republic](/wiki/Czech_Republic "Czech Republic"). It has about 200 inhabitants.

**Strukov** is a municipality and village in [Olomouc District](/wiki/Olomouc_District "Olomouc District") in the [Olomouc Region](/wiki/Olomouc_Region "Olomouc Region") of the [Czech Republic](/wiki/Czech_Republic "Czech Republic"). It has about 200 inhabitants.

## Geography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Strukov&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Geography")]

Strukov is located about 16 kilometres (10 mi) north of [Olomouc](/wiki/Olomouc "Olomouc"). It lies in a flat agricultural landscape in the [Upper Morava Valley](/wiki/Upper_Morava_Valley "Upper Morava Valley").

With an area of 0.53 square kilometres (0.20 sq mi), Strukov is the second smallest municipality in the country (after [Závist](/wiki/Z%C3%A1vist_(Blansko_District) "Závist (Blansko District)")).[[2]](#cite_note-2)

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Strukov&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: History")]

Strukov was founded in 1789.[[3]](#cite_note-3)

## Transport

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Strukov&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Transport")]

There are no railways or major roads passing through the municipality.

## Sights

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Strukov&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Sights")]

There are two protected cultural monuments in Strukov: a stone [conciliation cross](/wiki/Conciliation_cross "Conciliation cross"), which was created sometime in the 15th–17th century, and a house from the second half of the 19th century with a memorial plaque of historian and university professor František Hrubý, who was born there.[[6]](#cite_note-6)
